dimensions.label: Problem Clarity
dimensions.evidence: Problem statement names who (anyone resuming AI coding work on a repo), what (prior decisions, dead-ends, and open threads evaporate so context is re-explained), and the gap — `sessions` indexes history (cache.ts) but only retrieves and never re-injects.

dimensions.label: Goal Definition
dimensions.evidence: Four observable goals: a two-tier primer across all three tools and all worktrees, two surfaces sharing one extractor, a synthesis skill, and holding the no-LLM/no-network/no-new-deps moat.

dimensions.label: Success Criteria
dimensions.evidence: Nine criteria — eight carry runnable `bun test` / `grep` / `git diff` checks; the agent-synthesized-prose criterion is explicitly tagged a judgment call rather than given a fake check.

dimensions.label: Scope Boundaries
dimensions.evidence: MVP×9 nested in Full×3 nested in Stretch×2, with an explicit out-of-scope list (in-binary LLM, team/cloud layer, in-code decision detection) and a future axis.

dimensions.label: Consistency
dimensions.evidence: All four design forks resolved 2026-06-19 (all-3-tools file extraction, index-time precompute + SCHEMA_VERSION bump, aggregate-worktrees default, no validation gate); precompute satisfies the zero-file-read criterion and zod is already a dependency, so 'no new deps' holds.

problem: Every new AI coding session on a repo starts from zero. Prior decisions, dead-ends, and half-finished threads from past Claude Code / Codex / Pi sessions evaporate, so you re-explain context you already worked out. `sessions` already indexes that history (SQLite + FTS5 in cache.ts) but only does retrieval — search, resume, usage reports — and stops short of feeding context back into the next session.

This adds the missing re-injection layer: a repo-scoped 'context primer' surfacing what past-you decided, tried, and left open. To preserve the static-binary, no-LLM, no-network architecture (the project's actual moat), synthesis stays in the consuming agent via a skill; the binary only does deterministic extraction and ships the structured material.
goals: Produce a repo-scoped, two-tier context primer (recent sessions in detail + older sessions as dated headlines) from the existing index, across all three tools and across every worktree of a bare-repo project (aggregated by default, each branch-labeled).
Expose it on two surfaces sharing one extractor: a `get_context_primer` MCP tool (for in-agent synthesis) and a `sessions context` CLI command (for paste/pipe).
Ship a `context` skill that turns the structured data into a prose primer covering prior decisions, what was tried, and open threads.
Hold the line on the static-binary moat: no LLM call, no network, no new runtime dependencies.

successCriteria.criterion: Running `sessions context` in a repo with history prints a two-tier markdown primer — recent tier with intent / files / opening / closing (both roles) / meta per session, older tier as dated headline lines — bounded by a size cap.
successCriteria.check: bun test src/context.test.ts -t "two-tier" — exits 0 (asserts recent + headline tiers present and total output within the cap)

successCriteria.criterion: `get_context_primer` returns the same primer as zod-schema-validated JSON via the MCP server, following house style (JSON.stringify output, plain-string empty case).
successCriteria.check: bun test src/context.test.ts -t "mcp" — exits 0 (MCP handler output parses and matches the CLI extractor's structured data)

successCriteria.criterion: `extractFiles` correctly pulls edited file paths from Claude, Codex, and Pi session logs; one passing case per tool format (Claude Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit, Codex apply_patch/function-call, Pi).

Given the tool output, the `context` skill produces a primer that names prior decisions and flags the most-recent session's unfinished state as the open thread. Judgment call — agent-synthesized prose, verified by human review of a sample primer, not a mechanical test.

Run from any worktree of a bare-repo project, the primer aggregates sessions across all worktrees (each branch-labeled); resolution uses `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` and a boundary-aware cwd match so a sibling project like `dotfiles-v2` is never captured under `dotfiles`.
successCriteria.check: bun test src/context.test.ts -t "worktree" — exits 0 (fixture asserts cross-worktree aggregation with branch labels AND that a sibling `…-v2` cwd is excluded)

successCriteria.criterion: A repo with no sessions yields a graceful empty-state message rather than an error or stack trace.
successCriteria.check: bun test src/context.test.ts -t "empty-state" — exits 0 (empty-roots fixture returns the empty-state string with exit code 0)

successCriteria.criterion: After a SCHEMA_VERSION bump (2→3), `indexFile` stores `files_touched` and truncated closing messages (both roles) as columns, and `getContextPrimer` reads only those indexed columns — no per-invocation source-file reads.
successCriteria.check: bun test src/context.test.ts -t "indexed-columns" — exits 0 (asserts columns populated after refreshIndex, and an fs-read spy records zero source-file opens during getContextPrimer)

successCriteria.criterion: No new runtime dependency, no network call, and no LLM call is introduced — the static-binary moat holds.
successCriteria.check: git diff --stat package.json shows no added `dependencies`; grep -rnE 'fetch\(|https?://|anthropic|openai' src/context.ts src/extract-files.ts — no matches

successCriteria.criterion: The full quality gate passes — both the four CI jobs and the local test suite.
successCriteria.check: bun test — exits 0; bun run typecheck — exits 0; bun run lint — exits 0; bun run format:check — exits 0; bun run build — exits 0 (the last four mirror CI's lint/format/typecheck/compile jobs; `bun test` is a local-only gate since CI does not run tests)

mvp.reason: Recent N detailed + older headlines, repo-scoped via cwd prefix, reusing the queryDateRange pattern in cache.ts (created_at + tool + cwd LIKE filtering).

git-common-dir resolver replacing the brittle ../.git + .bare string match (cli.ts:79); boundary-aware cwd match fixing the confirmed sibling-capture bug at scanner.ts:43 and cache.ts:285/313; per-session branch labels; default aggregates all worktrees of a bare repo. Note: the resolver + boundary fix also change the existing `sessions` search/scan path (scanner.ts, cache.ts), not just the primer — intentional, and the existing search behavior must stay green.

mvp.item: Cross-tool extractFiles for Claude, Codex, and Pi
mvp.reason: Parse tool_use / apply_patch blocks for edited file paths — genuinely new (no tool-call parsing exists today; parser.ts drops all non-text blocks). Codex and Pi shapes are reverse-engineered from real logs — the project's top risk.

Store extracted file lists and truncated last user + last assistant text as columns during indexFile(), so the primer is a pure indexed query with zero per-call file I/O. One-time destructive reindex (PRAGMA user_version drops/rebuilds tables); also makes the digest's file list real.

mvp.item: Per-session data: intent, files, opening, closing (both roles)
mvp.reason: Closing captures last user AND last assistant message (stripped + truncated) — deliberately both, since last-assistant alone is often a question or tool-call, not an outcome. Served from indexed columns.

mvp.item: get_context_primer MCP tool (zod raw-shape, JSON output)
mvp.reason: Primary re-injection surface; agent calls it and synthesizes in-context. Mirrors the existing server.tool() raw-shape + JSON.stringify pattern in mcp.ts.

Renders the structured primer as markdown to stdout for paste/pipe into non-skill tools. Dispatch modeled on the `report` block in repo-root index.ts:41 (there is no src/index.ts); flags via a new src/context.ts parseContextArgs mirroring parseReportArgs in src/report/index.ts:45.
Instructs the agent to synthesize prose, anchoring open threads on the most-recent closing state. Mirrors plugin/skills/recall/SKILL.md structure (frontmatter + Steps + Guidelines).

mvp.item: Tests: per-tool extractFiles + fixture-based primer + CLI/MCP output
mvp.reason: Mirrors parser.test.ts (pure, inline-jsonl) and report/index.test.ts (temp-dir roots injection) patterns. Run locally with `bun test` — CI does not run tests.

full.reason: Tune recent-tier size, window, tool filter, detail depth, and --worktree to narrow from the project-wide aggregate to the current worktree only.
Graceful no-session message; bound the headline tier (e.g. 40 most-recent) so long-lived repos stay sized.

full.item: Plugin packaging: embed skill + advertise in `sessions setup`
full.reason: Regenerate the plugin-files embed (generate-plugin-embed) and add /context to the hardcoded setup skill list (setup.ts:198).

stretch.reason: Wire into `sessions setup` so the primer can appear automatically at session start — the full re-injection vision, paid for on every session.

outOfScope.reason: Rejected — breaks the static-binary / no-runtime moat. Synthesis lives in the consuming agent.
Nessie's multi-user direction; a different product, would blow up the local-CLI simplicity.

outOfScope.item: Deterministic decision / open-thread detection in code
outOfScope.reason: That synthesis is the agent's job via the skill, not the extractor's. The binary ships structured material only.

future: Topic-axis primer — cross-repo, organized by concept rather than repo.
Decision provenance extraction (what was decided and why).
Broader tool ingestion: Cursor, Windsurf, Aider, Gemini CLI logs.
Read-on-demand serving fallback — considered and rejected 2026-06-19 in favor of precompute; revisit if the destructive reindex proves too heavy for large corpora.

phases.title: Extraction core
phases.notes: Prereq: representative Codex (~/.codex/sessions) and Pi (~/.pi/agent/sessions) logs must be on hand as fixtures — their edited-file shapes exist nowhere in the codebase and must be reverse-engineered from real logs. Work: new types (ContextPrimer/ContextSession/ContextHeadline); cross-tool extractFiles (Claude tool_use + reverse-engineered Codex/Pi) in a new src/extract-files.ts (do NOT edit the vendored report/parsers/*); last-user + last-assistant closing-text stripping & truncation; SCHEMA_VERSION 2→3 storing files_touched + closing messages at index time (destructive reindex); git-common-dir repo resolver + boundary-aware cwd fix (scanner.ts:43, cache.ts:285/313) — must keep existing search tests green; branch-labeled two-tier getContextPrimer pure-SQL query in cache.ts. Pure-function + fixture tests. Rated high: Codex/Pi reverse-engineering and the destructive schema migration both land here.

phases.title: Surfaces + skill
phases.notes: get_context_primer MCP tool, sessions context CLI subcommand + markdown renderer + flags + help text, context SKILL.md, plugin embed regen + setup advertisement. CLI/MCP output tests.

phases.title: SessionStart auto-injection hook
phases.notes: Stretch. Opt-in SessionStart hook wired into `sessions setup --hooks`; idempotent settings-merge in hooks.ts; reuses `sessions context --hook` (tight defaults, never-throw). Default off, removable via `sessions uninstall`. Gated on confirming the Claude Code SessionStart hook contract (the phase's key unknown); Codex/Cursor support deferred until their hook schemas are verified.
